title: Lesson 387
transcript: Section 310, manage the information system lifecycle. What is the information system lifecycle? It's exactly what it sounds like. It's simply the entire lifespan of a system from its initial conception to its eventual decommissioning. The system lifecycle is just the entire lifecycle of a system. And here's the steps. It starts with stakeholders' needs and requirements, requirements analysis, architectural design, development and implementation, integration, validation and verification, in other words, a bunch of testing. deployments, ongoing maintenance and disposal. That's the entire lifecycle of the system. And I'm actually not going to spend too long talking about this. Here's why. This looks probably very familiar to you, this system lifecycle, to something you may have seen or you certainly will see in a bit in Domain 8. Here's the system or the software development lifecycle, right? It starts with initiation, then requirements, then architecture and design, then development, then testing, and then release. So it's the first part of this. And if you add on... The final two pieces here, so operation and disposal, that's the system life cycle. So the information system life cycle is essentially just the system life cycle. And we're gonna talk about, John's gonna talk about the system life cycle in great detail, or at least in a lot more detail, in domain eight and by the way there's just one thing i want to point out here is there's obviously an important difference or a difference here that integration isn't missed it mentioned down here in the system life cycle but Integration is simply where you're doing testing. So the point is this. The information system lifecycle is essentially the system lifecycle, which, like I said, John is going to cover in Domain 8. So that's why we're not really going to talk about this now. Easy section.
